input.headline: Is AI being shoved down your throat at work? Here’s how to fight back.

raw.headline: Is AI being shoved down your throat at work? Here’s how to fight back.

winner: Why did I start embracing AI at work after the article said to resist it? Because now I have a new way to procrastinate—blaming my lack of productivity on the algorithm.

candidates.text: I just asked my boss if we could switch to a slightly less advanced AI for our meetings. He said that would make us "less competitive." I suggested maybe we start using flip charts.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a relatable workplace scenario with a clever twist involving flip charts as a low-tech alternative to AI."}

candidates.text: My boss installed an AI that rewrites my emails before I send them, so now every "meeting" is called a "synergy session," and my "project update" is a "value proposition.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Specific, surprising, and concise with a clear connection to the headline; uses workplace humor effectively."}

candidates.text: My boss says I'm being forced to use AI—guess they want me to stop using my head and just start using my mouth!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Puns on 'using my head' vs. 'using my mouth' are clever and specific, with a surprising twist that aligns well with the headline's context."}

candidates.text: Why did I start embracing AI at work after the article said to resist it? Because now I have a new way to procrastinate—blaming my lack of productivity on the algorithm.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a surprising twist by flipping the article's advice into a humorous workaround. It naturally connects to the headline and is relatable in an office context."}

candidates.text: Why did the office printer start acting like a chatbot? It got *prompted* too much—now it’s asking for my feedback on every page!
